sections_cited: Section 236-K of the Income Tax Ordinance

chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI CP D 2317 of 2017 ____________________________________________________________ DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE ____________________________________________________________ 1. For hearing of CMA No.11434/2017 2. For hearing of CMA No.11435/2017 3. For hearing of main case 12.03.2025 Syed Mazharul Hassan, advocate for the petitioner Mr. Imran Ahmed Khan Abro, Assistant Advocate General The primary prayer clause in the petition seeks a direction to revenue not to collect tax under Section 236-K of the Income Tax Ordinance, 2001. Prima facie no reason would be articulated in such regard to be considered in writ jurisdiction. The remaining two prayer clauses also challenge the quantum and recovery of C.V.T. While the petitioner remained at liberty to agitate any grievance in such regard before the competent authority / statutory hierarchy no case is made out for direct recourse to writ jurisdiction. This petition, pending since 2017, is found to be misconceived, hence, dismissed. Judge Judge Amjad
